headline: INDIA FREEZES BULLION IMPORTS: 5 TONNES GOLD, 8 TONNES SILVER STRANDED AT CUSTOMS BEFORE FESTIVAL SEASON
snippet: Indian banks have halted fresh bullion orders after the DGFT missed its April 1 deadline to renew annual import authorisations. More than 5 tonnes of gold and 8 tonnes of silver are stranded at customs, accruing storage and insurance costs with no clearance in sight. The freeze arrives weeks before Akshaya Tritiya — the world's second-largest seasonal gold demand event — and adds a potential supply-crunch variable that global bullion markets have not priced in.

headline: THE RESERVE CRISIS: MAJORS ARE MINING FASTER THAN THEY CAN DISCOVER — JUNIORS WITH DRILL PROGRAMS ARE THE FIX
snippet: Grassroots exploration budgets have collapsed to historic lows while the world's biggest gold miners deplete reserves faster than they can replace them — and new discoveries still take over a decade to reach production. Central banks are absorbing roughly 850 tonnes per year, tightening the supply gap that majors cannot close alone. The structural case for quality junior explorers running systematic drill programs in proven districts has rarely been cleaner.

headline: WEST RED LAKE GOLD (TSXV: WRLG) DRILLS 30.59 G/T AU AT ROWAN, EXTENDS VEINS BEYOND PEA MINE PLAN
snippet: West Red Lake Gold confirmed high-grade continuity at its Rowan mine in Ontario's Red Lake district with 30.59 g/t Au over 1.0m (Vein 001) and 19.83 g/t Au over 1.62m (Vein 013). The 37-hole infill program extends Veins 006b and 013 beyond zones included in the 2025 PEA, potentially increasing daily mined tonnage materially. A combined Madsen-Rowan pre-feasibility study is targeted for Q3 2026.

headline: ELDORADO GOLD CLOSES $3.8B FORAN MINING DEAL — ADDS MCILVENNA BAY COPPER-ZINC PROJECT IN SASKATCHEWAN
snippet: Eldorado Gold completed its $3.8 billion acquisition of Foran Mining on April 14, making Foran wholly owned and adding the McIlvenna Bay copper-zinc project in Saskatchewan to Eldorado's asset base. Both shareholder meetings voted yes by supermajority (84% EGO, 83% FOM). The deal signals that mid-tier gold miners are willing to pay premium multiples for undeveloped base-metal projects — a direct read-through for junior Cu-Zn explorers.

headline: SOUTH32/TECK/SPROTT-BACKED AMERICAN EAGLE GOLD LAUNCHES $31M HOSTILE TENDER FOR PACIFIC BOOKER MINERALS
snippet: American Eagle Gold — backed by miners South32 and Teck alongside financier Eric Sprott — launched an unsolicited $31 million tender offer for Pacific Booker Minerals this week, with shareholders having until July 29 to tender their shares. The move illustrates how strategically backed acquirers are targeting undervalued small-cap copper juniors at what they view as a structural entry point ahead of the expected supply-demand crunch.

headline: COPPER HOLDS NEAR TWO-MONTH HIGHS AT $6.10/LB — AI BUILDOUT, DEFENSE SURGE AND 15% U.S. TARIFF THREAT CONVERGE
snippet: Copper futures reclaimed two-month highs near $6.10/lb this week, up roughly 30% year-over-year, as three demand drivers converged: AI data centre expansion requiring massive wiring, a global defence build-up, and anticipation of a 15% U.S. tariff on refined copper imports. The LME all-time high of $13,387.50/tonne is now within reach as structural supply deficits widen and major undeveloped projects remain years from production.

headline: KOBOLD METALS LAUNCHES WORLD'S LARGEST EVER LITHIUM EXPLORATION CAMPAIGN — $50M, 3,000 SQ KM IN DRC
snippet: AI-driven exploration firm KoBold Metals has launched what it calls the largest lithium exploration campaign in history: 13 licences covering 3,000+ square kilometres in the Democratic Republic of Congo, backed by over $50 million in funding through early 2027. The Manono area — home to some of the world's highest-grade lithium pegmatites — is at the centre of the program. KoBold will use proprietary aerial sensors and real-time AI targeting across 30,000 sq km of airborne surveys.

headline: FORTUNA MINING RENEWS 5% BUYBACK AT RECORD GOLD PRICES — SIGNALS FREE CASH FLOW SURPLUS FOR MID-TIER MINERS
snippet: Fortuna Mining's board approved the renewal of its normal course issuer bid, authorising repurchase of up to 15.2 million shares — 5% of shares outstanding. The renewal arrives as gold trades above $4,800/oz, putting mid-tier producers in a rare position of substantial free cash flow generation. Fortuna's buyback is the clearest on-the-ground signal yet that diversified precious metals miners are returning capital rather than chasing growth spending.

headline: CAMECO UP 4.2% AS URANIUM LONG-TERM CONTRACTING STRENGTHENS — TRADETEK LONG-TERM PRICE UP TO $93/LB
snippet: Cameco (CCJ/CCO) gained 4.2% on April 15 as uranium market sentiment improved on firmer long-term contracting activity. TradeTech's monthly Long-Term Uranium Price Indicator moved higher to US$93.00/lb as of March 31. Kazakhstan supply uncertainty continues to tighten the picture for incumbents with reliable contracted capacity. NexGen Energy's Rook I — the largest undeveloped uranium deposit in Canada — is targeting summer 2026 groundbreaking, adding to the development pipeline narrative.

headline: RIO TINTO'S $2B U.S. BORON ASSETS DRAW 12+ BIDDERS — CRITICAL MINERAL SALE TESTS STRATEGIC VALUE OF CALIFORNIA DEPOSITS
snippet: Rio Tinto has attracted more than a dozen potential buyers for its U.S. boron business — a Mojave Desert mine, a Los Angeles refinery, and Sierra Nevada operations — valued at approximately $2 billion, with binding offers expected by June. Known bidders include WE Soda, Magris Resources, and Apollo-controlled US Silica Holdings. The sale is part of Rio's strategic pivot to focus on iron ore, copper, aluminium, and lithium, underscoring how critical mineral assets command premium valuations even within disposals.
